hypothesis: Incorporating temporal degradation trajectories of electrolyte formulations—captured as time-series embeddings of capacity fade and impedance growth across battery cycling—into the pre-training corpus of SMILES-based foundation models will enable those models to learn thermodynamic instability signatures at the molecular level, such that the resulting embeddings will predict long-term cycle life (>500 cycles) with lower out-of-distribution error than models trained solely on static molecular or formulation representations, because the latent space will encode kinetic decomposition pathways of chemical moieties rather than only equilibrium structural features.
